methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Jimmy Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Antoine Guyot and Mathilde Grivet that has raised €80M to date. The valuation universe for nuclear energy comprises 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers, including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains insufficient narrow-stage comparables—only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer were identified against a threshold of 3 required datapoints. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in capital intensity, technology maturity, and commercialization timelines across the broader nuclear cohort. A focused triangulation approach using public market multiples and transaction precedents within nuclear energy infrastructure would be more defensible, though execution requires granular disclosure of public peer revenue visibility and private transaction details not present in this dataset.

The analysis cannot support a specific valuation range without additional quantitative inputs: revenue or EBITDA figures for Jimmy Energy and selected peers, EV/revenue or EV/EBITDA multiples from the public tranche, and pricing details from recent Series A or growth-stage nuclear energy financings. Key uncertainties include technology stage differentiation among peers, path to commercialization timelines, and regulatory capital requirements. Further data collection on peer financial metrics and transaction terms is necessary to triangulate a defensible valuation.
